questions.stem: In a RAG interview, what is the core idea that distinguishes RAG from pure generation?
options: Retrieve external evidence and condition the LLM on it before answering
Fine-tune the LLM nightly on every new document in the enterprise
Replace embeddings with random vectors to increase answer diversity
Disable the context window so the model must memorize all facts
questions.explanation: RAG grounds answers in fetched passages rather than parameters alone.

questions.stem: Chunking strategy: why does naive fixed-size splitting often underperform in RAG?
options: Fixed sizes guarantee optimal recall for every query distribution
It can break semantic units so retrieval returns incomplete or split context
Chunking is unnecessary because models read entire corpora per request
Only overlapping chunks are illegal in vector databases by design
questions.explanation: Meaningful boundaries (sections/paragraphs) usually beat blind character cuts.

questions.stem: Embeddings in RAG: what role do they play in the retrieval pipeline primarily?
options: Replace the generator LLM so answers are nearest-neighbor strings only
Compress PDFs into a single scalar relevance score without vectors
Map queries and chunks into a vector space for similarity search
Train the reranker using next-token prediction exclusively
questions.explanation: Dense vectors enable semantic matching beyond exact keyword overlap.

questions.stem: Dense versus sparse retrieval: how should you contrast them in an interview?
options: Sparse always needs GPUs; dense only needs inverted indexes on CPU
They produce identical rankings for every query by construction
Dense retrieval cannot handle multilingual text while BM25 can only
Dense uses embeddings/semantics; sparse uses term statistics like BM25
questions.explanation: Different matching signals: meaning similarity versus lexical overlap.

questions.stem: Hybrid search: what combination and benefit should you articulate clearly for RAG?
options: Fuse dense and sparse scores to catch both semantic and exact matches
Run two generators and average their tokens without any retrieval
Hybrid means storing vectors only and deleting the keyword index
It forbids reranking because fusion already yields perfect order
questions.explanation: Lexical + semantic retrieval often improves robustness on mixed queries.

questions.stem: Reranking in RAG: where does it sit in the pipeline and why do teams use it?
options: Before indexing, to delete all documents that mention numbers
After cheap retrieval, a stronger model reorders top candidates more accurately
Instead of generation, returning the top reranked chunk as the final answer
Only during fine-tuning and never at query time in production
questions.explanation: Cross-encoders/rerankers trade compute for better precision@k.

questions.stem: Faithfulness metrics: what are they trying to measure in RAG system evaluation?
options: How fast the vector index returns the first candidate document
Whether the embedding model was trained with contrastive loss
Whether the generated answer is supported by the retrieved context
The BLEU score between two random chunks in the corpus
questions.explanation: Faithfulness checks grounding: claims should be entailed by evidence.

questions.stem: RAG versus fine-tuning for knowledge: which decision heuristic is interview-solid?
options: Always fine-tune because RAG cannot cite sources by definition
Always RAG because fine-tuning cannot change model behavior at all
They are mutually exclusive and cannot be combined in one system
Prefer RAG for mutable/citable facts; fine-tune for style/skills/format
questions.explanation: Knowledge freshness and attribution favor RAG; behavior shaping favors FT.

questions.stem: Metadata filters with vector search: when do they matter in enterprise RAG?
options: Restrict retrieval by tenant, time, ACL, or doc type before/after ANN search
Metadata replaces embeddings so vectors become unnecessary forever
Filters only apply to BM25 and cannot combine with dense retrieval
ACLs should be enforced only in the LLM prompt, never in the index
questions.explanation: Security and relevance often require structured constraints plus vectors.

questions.stem: Query rewriting / HyDE-style tricks: what problem are they trying to fix in RAG?
options: They delete the need for an embedding model in the RAG stack
User queries may be short/ambiguous; expanded queries retrieve better chunks
Rewriting guarantees zero hallucinations without any citations
HyDE only works for SQL databases and not document corpora
questions.explanation: Better queries improve recall when raw user text mismatches corpus style.

questions.stem: Top-k retrieval: what tradeoff should you mention when choosing k for RAG context?
options: k must equal the embedding dimension for mathematical correctness
k=1 is always optimal because rerankers cannot use more candidates
Larger k raises recall but adds noise/cost and may distract the LLM
Top-k only applies to sparse search and not to dense ANN indexes
questions.explanation: Context quality vs coverage is the central top-k design tension.

questions.stem: Indexing freshness: how do you keep RAG answers current as documents change?
options: Fine-tune the generator nightly instead of updating the vector store
Never update the index; rely on the LLM’s parametric knowledge only
Freshness is automatic because cosine similarity tracks wall-clock time
Re-embed/upsert changed chunks and delete stale IDs on a reliable pipeline
questions.explanation: Document lifecycle must sync into the retrieval index continuously.

questions.stem: Citation / attribution UX: why do interviewers care about grounded citations in RAG?
options: Users and auditors need to verify which passages support each claim
Citations replace the generator so retrieval alone answers every query
Showing sources always increases hallucination rates by construction
Attribution is only required for BM25 and never for dense RAG
questions.explanation: Traceability builds trust and enables error analysis of bad retrievals.

questions.stem: Embedding model choice: what properties should you evaluate for RAG retrieval quality?
options: Only the parameter count of the generator LLM matters for retrieval
Domain fit, chunk length behavior, multilingual support, and retrieval benchmarks
Any random projection matches SOTA embedders on MTEB-style tasks
Embedders trained only on images are best for pure text corpora
questions.explanation: Retrieval quality hinges on the embedding space matching your content.

questions.stem: Overlap between chunks: what is the usual motivation for overlapping windows in RAG?
options: Guarantee that every token appears in exactly one chunk globally
Overlap forces BM25 scores to become identical to dense scores
Preserve sentences/entities that would otherwise be split across boundaries
It reduces index size by storing each span multiple times less often
questions.explanation: Overlaps reduce edge artifacts when evidence straddles chunk cuts.

questions.stem: ANN indexes (HNSW/IVF): what compromise do approximate indexes introduce for search?
options: Exact search at lower latency than brute force without any tradeoff
ANN indexes only store keywords and cannot store dense vectors
They require rewriting the LLM architecture to include HNSW layers
Faster/cheaper search with possible recall loss versus exact nearest neighbors
questions.explanation: Approximation trades a bit of recall for scalable vector search.

questions.stem: Context compression / map-reduce RAG: when do these patterns become useful?
options: When retrieved text exceeds the window or is too noisy for one shot
Only when the corpus has fewer than ten documents total
Compression deletes the need for embeddings by hashing queries
Map-reduce RAG forbids citations and multi-document synthesis
questions.explanation: Summarize/select evidence so generation sees high-signal context.

questions.stem: Evaluation of retrieval: which metrics belong in a solid RAG interview answer?
options: Only train loss of the generator without looking at retrieved docs
Recall@k, MRR/nDCG, and downstream answer faithfulness/correctness
Silhouette score of embedding clusters as the sole RAG KPI
GPU utilization percentage during ANN search exclusively
questions.explanation: Measure both retrieval ranking quality and end-to-end answer quality.

questions.stem: Multi-hop questions in RAG: why do single-shot top-k pipelines often fail?
options: Multi-hop is impossible for Transformers even with perfect retrieval
Increasing k to corpus size always solves multi-hop without iteration
Evidence is split across docs; needs iterative retrieval or decomposition
BM25 cannot return more than one document per query by design
questions.explanation: Complex questions require chaining retrieval steps over partial answers.

questions.stem: Prompting the generator with retrieved docs: what failure mode should you watch?
options: Models always quote retrieved text verbatim with perfect attribution
Retrieved context cannot influence decoding once temperature is set
Prompt order never affects which passages the model attends to
Ignoring context, contradicting sources, or blending unsourced parametric knowledge
questions.explanation: Grounding instructions and citations reduce unsupported generations.

questions.stem: Graph RAG at a high level: what extra structure does it exploit beyond text chunks?
options: Entities/relations graphs to traverse connected facts for multi-hop reasoning
Only pixel graphs from screenshots of PDFs without text extraction
Replacing vector search with random walks that ignore the user query
Graphs delete the need for an LLM generator in the answering stage
questions.explanation: Knowledge graphs help assemble evidence paths sparse chunk search misses.

questions.stem: Parent-document / small-to-big retrieval: what idea improves generation context?
options: Always embed entire books as one vector for maximum precision@1
Retrieve small chunks for precision, then expand to larger parent context
Delete parent documents so only orphan sentences remain searchable
Small-to-big means training a smaller LLM to replace the larger one
questions.explanation: Fine-grained matching plus broader context often improves answers.

questions.stem: Late chunking / contextualized chunk embeddings: what problem do they target?
options: They remove positional information so all chunks share one embedding
Late chunking forbids hybrid search by architectural constraint
Chunks lose document context; contextualization enriches chunk vectors
It only applies to image patches and not to text corpora
questions.explanation: Better chunk vectors reduce ambiguity from isolated spans.

questions.stem: ACL-aware RAG: how should retrieval respect document permissions correctly?
options: Put all secret docs in the prompt and ask the model not to leak them
Encrypt vectors but allow any authenticated user to retrieve any ID
ACLs are unnecessary if the LLM was RLHF’d for confidentiality
Filter candidates by the user’s entitlements before returning context
questions.explanation: Authorization must be enforced in the retrieval layer, not by hope.

questions.stem: Reranker distillation / cheaper rerankers: why might teams train a student model?
options: Retain most quality of a cross-encoder at lower latency and cost
Students always beat teachers on nDCG without any labeled pairs
Distillation removes the first-stage retriever from the pipeline
Rerankers cannot be distilled because ranking is not differentiable
questions.explanation: Latency budgets often require lighter second-stage rankers.

questions.stem: Negative sampling for embedding training: why do hard negatives matter for retrieval?
options: Negatives must be random noise vectors with zero text content only
They teach the model to separate near-miss distractors from true hits
Hard negatives reduce the need for any positive query-doc pairs
Embedding models cannot use negatives because loss is unsupervised only
questions.explanation: Informative negatives sharpen the retrieval decision boundary.

questions.stem: Routing between RAG and parametric answers: when might you skip retrieval?
options: Never retrieve because parametric memory is always fresher than indexes
Always retrieve the entire corpus for every greeting message
Chitchat/skills tasks where docs add noise; retrieve for knowledge questions
Routing is impossible without fine-tuning a new tokenizer
questions.explanation: Query classification avoids unnecessary retrieval latency and distraction.

questions.stem: Table/PDF parsing quality: how does it affect downstream RAG system performance?
options: Parsing quality is irrelevant if the generator is large enough
Vector search repairs OCR errors via cosine similarity automatically
PDFs cannot be chunked; only plain .txt files are valid RAG sources
Broken extraction yields junk chunks that embeddings cannot magically fix
questions.explanation: Garbage-in retrieval starts at ingestion/layout understanding.

questions.stem: Self-reflective / corrective RAG loops: what do they attempt after first retrieval?
options: Critique relevance/support and retrieve again if evidence is insufficient
Delete the index and fine-tune whenever a user downvotes an answer
Always accept the first top-1 chunk without checking support
Reflection replaces embeddings with reinforcement learning policies only
questions.explanation: Iterative critique can recover from weak initial retrievals.

questions.stem: Chunk size vs embedding model context: what mismatch should you carefully avoid?
options: Larger chunks always embed better regardless of model limits
Chunks longer than the embedder’s useful window get truncated/poor vectors
Embedders ignore length and hash all text to a 32-bit integer
Chunk size must equal the generator’s context window exactly
questions.explanation: Align chunking with the embedding model’s effective input limits.

questions.stem: MMR (maximal marginal relevance): what retrieval issue does diversification address?
options: Maximizes only lexical overlap while ignoring semantic diversity
Forces all retrieved chunks to come from the same parent document
Diversifies results so top-k is not near-duplicate redundant passages
MMR replaces generation with extractive QA without an LLM
questions.explanation: Redundancy reduction improves information coverage in the prompt.

questions.stem: Online evaluation for RAG: what signals complement offline nDCG/faithfulness?
options: Only GPU temperature and fan speed during ANN queries
Offline metrics fully determine production quality with zero drift
A/B tests are illegal for retrieval systems in enterprise settings
User thumbs, escalation rates, citation clicks, and grounded answer audits
questions.explanation: Production feedback catches failures offline golden sets miss.

questions.stem: Multi-vector / ColBERT-style late interaction: what idea improves matching quality?
options: Keep token-level vectors and interact late for finer relevance scoring
Collapse each document to one bit and rank by Hamming distance only
Forbid dense features and use only unigram BM25 forever
Late interaction means running the generator before retrieval
questions.explanation: Fine-grained interactions can beat single-vector similarity on tough queries.

questions.stem: Knowledge base versioning: why version documents and embeddings together in RAG?
options: Versioning is optional because cosine similarity is deterministic forever
Reproduce answers, roll back bad ingestions, and audit what was retrievable
Only version the LLM weights and never the document store
Embeddings cannot be versioned because vectors are continuous
questions.explanation: Reproducibility and ops safety need pinned corpus+index snapshots.

questions.stem: Prompt injection via retrieved documents: what defense themes should you list?
options: Trust all indexed text because indexing implies safety review
Disable citations so injected instructions cannot be traced
Treat docs as untrusted data, sanitize, and isolate tool permissions
Injection only affects chat UIs and never RAG pipelines
questions.explanation: Retrieved content can contain hostile instructions; bound their power.

questions.stem: Synthetic query generation for eval/training: what caution should you raise?
options: Synthetic queries always perfectly mirror production traffic by theory
You cannot evaluate RAG without synthetic queries exclusively
Generated queries make faithfulness metrics mathematically undefined
Synthetics boost coverage but may not match real user query distributions
questions.explanation: Validate that synthetic distributions don’t create illusory gains.

questions.stem: Agentic RAG versus classic single-shot RAG: what capability is added by agents?
options: The system can plan, call tools, and retrieve iteratively toward a goal
Agentic RAG removes the LLM and answers with SQL only
Single-shot RAG already plans multi-step tool use by definition
Agentic means storing graphs instead of vectors with no LLM calls
questions.explanation: Autonomy and multi-step control extend beyond one retrieve-then-read.

questions.stem: When do you add a knowledge graph on top of vector RAG in production practice?
options: Always, because graphs strictly dominate vectors for every query type
When relations, hierarchies, or multi-hop entity paths dominate the workload
Never, because graphs cannot store textual evidence for citations
Only when the corpus is smaller than the embedding dimension
questions.explanation: Graph structure helps when connectivity matters more than bag similarity.

questions.stem: Reranking features beyond cross-encoders: what else might score RAG candidates?
options: Only the document ID alphabetical order is a valid production signal
Reranking forbids using any signal other than raw BM25
Metadata boosts, freshness, click models, and LLM-as-judge relevance scores
Features must be identical to the embedding dimensions always
questions.explanation: Production rankers often blend learned and business relevance signals.

questions.stem: Failure analysis playbook: an answer is wrong — how do you triage RAG bugs?
options: Always retrain the LLM first because retrieval cannot be the cause
Increase temperature until the wrong answer becomes a right answer
Delete faithfulness metrics so wrong answers no longer fail evals
Check retrieval miss vs bad chunking vs generator ignoring good context
questions.explanation: Localize errors to retrieve, rank, or generate stages systematically.

questions.stem: Senior RAG design: how do you set SLOs that balance quality, latency, and cost?
options: Define answer quality gates, p95 latency, and $/query budgets with tradeoff knobs
Optimize only for maximum k and largest models without latency caps
SLOs are unnecessary if offline nDCG looks strong on a tiny golden set
Cost is minimized by retrieving the entire corpus on every request
questions.explanation: Product SLOs force explicit tradeoffs among the three axes.

questions.stem: When is fine-tuning an embedding model worth it versus a strong off-the-shelf one?
options: Always — general embedders cannot work on any specialized corpus
Domain jargon/query style mismatches where labeled pairs show clear gains
Never — fine-tuned embedders always overfit and lose all recall
Only when you lack any documents to index in the vector store
questions.explanation: Domain adaptation helps when generic similarity spaces misrank.

questions.stem: Cross-encoder everywhere vs retrieve-then-rerank: why not score the full corpus?
options: Cross-encoders are faster than ANN and should replace indexes always
Rerankers cannot improve precision@k after a weak first stage
Cross-encoders are too slow/expensive to score millions of docs online
Full-corpus cross-encoding is required for ACLs to work correctly
questions.explanation: Two-stage pipelines exist because expressive scorers don’t scale alone.

questions.stem: How should you evaluate Graph RAG differently from vanilla chunk-based RAG?
options: Only measure BM25 on node labels and ignore graph edges completely
Graph RAG cannot be evaluated because graphs lack textual evidence
Use image classification accuracy as the primary Graph RAG metric
Measure path correctness, entity linking quality, and multi-hop answer support
questions.explanation: Graph-specific failure modes need entity/relation-aware metrics.

questions.stem: Tenant isolation in multi-tenant vector DBs: what must never happen in RAG?
options: One tenant’s query retrieving another tenant’s chunks via shared indexes
Each tenant having a separate namespace or hard filter on tenant_id
Encrypting payloads at rest while still filtering by tenant metadata
Auditing retrieval logs tagged with tenant and user identifiers
questions.explanation: Hard isolation/filtering is mandatory for secure multi-tenant RAG.

questions.stem: Long-context models vs RAG: does a huge window make retrieval obsolete entirely?
options: Yes — any context window over 100k tokens removes all need for indexes
No — ranking/cost/freshness/ACLs still favor selective retrieval for large corpora
Yes — long context guarantees perfect attention over millions of tokens
No — because long-context models cannot read retrieved passages at all
questions.explanation: Windows help, but corpora and constraints still need retrieval systems.

questions.stem: Calibration of retrieval confidence: how can low-confidence handling help UX?
options: Always answer confidently even when no document scores above noise
Hide uncertainty because users cannot understand confidence concepts
Abstain, ask clarifying questions, or broaden search when scores are weak
Confidence is undefined for hybrid search by mathematical law
questions.explanation: Knowing when you don’t know prevents fluent but ungrounded answers.

questions.stem: Build vs buy for vector DB + pipelines: what decision factors matter most for RAG?
options: Always build because managed services cannot store embeddings legally
Always buy because open-source indexes cannot do ANN search
The choice depends only on which vendor has the flashiest demo
Scale, ops skill, SLA, features (hybrid/ACL), and lock-in versus control
questions.explanation: Infrastructure decisions should follow workload and team constraints.

questions.stem: How do you prevent eval contamination when using LLMs as faithfulness judges?
options: Blind studies, diverse judges, gold human labels, and adversarial checks
Use the same generator as the only judge without any human audits
Judges should see the gold answer key inside the system prompt always
Faithfulness judges are immune to bias if temperature is zero
questions.explanation: LLM judges help at scale but need calibration against humans.

questions.stem: Personalization in RAG: what privacy and quality tensions should you call out?
options: Personalization requires storing plaintext passwords in the vector index
User history can improve ranking but raises privacy, leakage, and bias risks
Privacy is unaffected because embeddings cannot reconstruct text ever
Personalized RAG forbids ACLs because profiles override permissions
questions.explanation: Use minimal necessary profile signals with strong isolation controls.
